items.quote: I'd obviously have to present it to ownership
0 OpportunityContactRole records exist for this opportunity
No process, timeline, or committee documented beyond 'present to ownership'
Competitors__c blank, but status-quo tools (Smartsheet, Procore, Jonas) and a live Procore-vs-Zycus question are documented in the call
No dollar figure or savings target has been attached to the described pain

metrics.narrative: No quantified value case exists yet. The buyer has described the operational pain (manual invoice entry, eight disconnected tools, no project-level spend visibility) but has not attached a dollar figure or savings target to it (call, 26 Mar 2026). Zycus's own ballpark pricing ($100k-$200k/yr plus a roughly matching one-time implementation fee, sent by Bailey Erazo via email 26 Mar 2026) has not yet been tested against any buyer-side budget or ROI threshold.

champion.narrative: Kyle Campbell is an engaged, positive contact but not yet a proven champion. He closed the 26 Mar 2026 call saying 'you guys do have my interest... something that could benefit us,' and asked for demo videos and pricing specifically so he could pitch it to 'ownership.' He has gone quiet since asking to push the follow-up from 23 Apr to 7 May 2026 (Task 00TP700000m8GAiMAM); that rescheduled meeting was itself cancelled, and there is no evidence yet he has taken the pitch upstairs.

competition.narrative: No named competitor is on record (Competitors__c and Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c both blank). The live alternative is inertia: Bronte's current stack of Smartsheet, Procore, and Jonas. Kyle Campbell is separately weighing whether Procore's own procurement extensions could cover enough ground to avoid adding a ninth system ('if I need to install another software to make our job better, it is what it is... but I am curious,' call, 26 Mar 2026).

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is clearly and directly buyer-voiced. Kyle Campbell described procurement as under a year old at Bronte, running on Smartsheet ('like Excel on steroids') as a stand-in ERP, using eight separate tools including Jonas for budgeting, and manually keying every vendor invoice into the ERP; he also flagged union-compliance risk in vendor sourcing as a recurring operational headache (call, 26 Mar 2026). This is confirmed, specific, and owned by the one identified stakeholder.

sources: No contracting fields populated; Opportunity stage = Qualified
paper_process.narrative: No paper process discussion has occurred. The deal has not reached commercials or contracting; no MSA, order form, or contract-expiration forcing function has been raised in any call or field. This is expected at this early stage.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer or budget owner is identified. Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c and Decision_Maker_Identified__c are both false and Who_will_approve_budget__c is blank. On the 26 Mar 2026 call, Kyle Campbell (Manager, Procurement) said that if the fit looks right he would 'have to present it to ownership' before any commitment, meaning budget authority sits above him and has had zero contact, direct or indirect, to date.

decision_process.narrative: The purchase process is undocumented. The only process signal is Kyle Campbell's own comment that he would need to present the solution 'to ownership' before it could move forward (call, 26 Mar 2026), implying at least one additional, unidentified approval layer above the procurement manager. No timeline, committee, or sequence of approvals has been confirmed.

decision_criteria.narrative: Only informal signals exist. On the 26 Mar 2026 call, Kyle Campbell repeatedly returned to two evaluation threads: whether Zycus can track spend at the individual-project level (vs Jonas/Procore) and whether the AI sourcing engine can reliably exclude non-union vendors from RFx events. Neither is captured as a formal Business_Requirements__c or ShoeFit criterion (both blank/false in Salesforce).

items.name: Reschedule request from Kyle Campbell
items.summary: Kyle Campbell emailed Bailey Erazo the morning of the meeting asking to push it to May 7th due to a conflict; Bailey confirmed the new date, so no discussion happened today.

items.name: Bronte Construction - Zycus | Follow Up (rescheduled)
items.summary: The scheduled follow-up did not take place as planned — it was rescheduled to May 7th per Kyle's last-minute request, so no substance was covered.

day_summary.overall: A follow-up meeting scheduled for today got bumped at the last minute — Kyle Campbell asked that morning to push it to May 7th since he couldn't attend, and Bailey Erazo confirmed the reschedule. Net effect is a two-week slip on this touchpoint with nothing substantive discussed today; the ball is now sitting on the calendar for May 7th.

cro_panel.intro: Early-stage, single-threaded Qualified deal with one strong discovery call and real, buyer-voiced pain, but 105 days without stage movement, no economic buyer contact, and a cancelled follow-up that has not been rebooked

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

bullets.full: Single-threaded through Kyle Campbell (role unknown). No multi-threading, no buying committee mapped, no internal champion. Deal will die if Kyle leaves or deprioritizes.
Zero MEDDPICC coverage. No quantified value case, no decision criteria, no competitive intelligence, no champion, no pain owner. Deal lacks all foundational qualification elements.

deal_movement.summary: Opportunity created directly into Qualified on 26 Mar 2026 following the intro/demo call; no stage or amount movement since. The only forward motion has been email/deliverable exchange, and the sole scheduled follow-up was rescheduled once then cancelled.

forecast_read.math: 76 days to close (today 2026-07-09 to 2026-09-23 close). 105 days in Qualified stage since Qualified_Submission_Date__c of 2026-03-26 against an expected ~30-day Qualified-stage cadence. Last verified buyer activity 2026-04-23, 77 days ago.
forecast_read.reason: Stage and recorded forecast both reflect an early, unproven deal; nothing in the evidence (no economic buyer contact, 77 days of buyer silence, cancelled follow-up) supports moving beyond Pipeline.

items.detail: 78 days to close with zero stage progression, 75-day buyer silence, and no demos or workshops completed. September 23 close is mathematically impossible at current velocity.
No EB, no DM, no executive sponsor identified. For a $120k P2P deal, CFO or VP Finance sign-off is required, but no executive engagement has occurred.
Single-threaded through Kyle Campbell (role unknown). No multi-threading, no buying committee mapped, no internal champion. Deal will die if Kyle leaves or deprioritizes.
Zero MEDDPICC coverage. No quantified value case, no decision criteria, no competitive intelligence, no champion, no pain owner. Deal lacks all foundational qualification elements.

Ballpark pricing of $100k-$200k/yr plus a roughly matching one-time implementation fee was sent 26 Mar 2026 but has drawn no buyer reaction in 105 days; affordability fit for a company Kyle described as still building out its procurement function is untested.
No confirmed prior Procore integration exists; Bailey Erazo could not verify one on the call and it remains an open action item.
No economic buyer or executive sponsor identified; Kyle Campbell must present internally to 'ownership' before any budget decision.
Single-threaded through one procurement manager with no confirmed internal coalition or sponsor; deal is exposed if Kyle Campbell deprioritizes or moves on.
Kyle Campbell cited fiscal year-end and inventory-management workload as reasons for delay in late March/April 2026; unclear whether that has cleared given the extended 77-day silence.

critical_signals.text: The one deep discovery-and-demo call (26 Mar 2026, about 15 weeks ago) landed well, with the buyer asking detailed questions about union-vendor filtering, project-level spend tracking, and AI negotiation, and closing on a positive note. But the planned follow-up was pushed from 23 Apr to 7 May 2026 at the buyer's own request and then cancelled, with no new date set since.

Ballpark pricing ($100k-$200k a year for 25-30 users plus a roughly matching one-time implementation fee) and the demo video package were both sent the same day as the intro call (26 Mar 2026) but have drawn no buyer reaction in the 105 days since.
The deal remains single-threaded through Kyle Campbell, Manager of Procurement, with zero other contacts logged and no Salesforce contact role recorded for him at all.

No named rival is in the picture; the real competition is the buyer's own patchwork of Smartsheet, Procore, and Jonas, and a live internal question about whether Procore's own extensions could absorb the need instead of adding Zycus.

champion_strength.summary: Kyle Campbell showed genuine, specific interest and explicit intent to advocate internally ('present it to ownership'), but there is no confirmation that advocacy has happened, and he has not responded since the 7 May 2026 meeting was cancelled.

expansion_context.note: No sibling Closed-Won opportunity on record for Bronte Construction.

confidence_signals.summary: The deal has cooled sharply since a strong initial call; the one confirmed contact has gone silent for 77 days after personally requesting to push out the only scheduled follow-up, which was then cancelled outright.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-03-26, now 103 days old. In Qualified stage the entire cycle with no progression to Formal Evaluation or Shortlisted. Last buyer activity 2026-04-23 (75 days ago). Close date 2026-09-23 is 78 days out. NextStep references April follow-up that never happened. At current zero velocity, deal will not reach Vendor Selected (typically 8-10 months into cycle) before close date. No submission dates recorded so true stage velocity cannot be computed from milestones. Forward slip risk is extreme.

north_star_verdict.headline: Deal is cold with 75-day buyer silence and remains in Qualified stage 103 days after creation with 78 days to close; no demos completed, no champion mapped, no economic buyer identified, and zero progression toward the September close date.

flags: Zero MEDDPICC coverage: all eight elements are knowledge gaps
No champion, no EB, no DM identified across all sources

best_practice_check.summary: The two highest-leverage moves are fixing the single-thread/contact-role hygiene gap and re-establishing a live next step after the cancelled meeting; both are prerequisites to any credible progress toward the 23 Sep 2026 close.

customer_preference.evidence: No preference statement has been made; the buyer's only stated intent (call, 26 Mar 2026) is to review materials before presenting internally to ownership.

competitive_position.summary: No named competitor is in play. The real contest is against inertia: Bronte's existing patchwork of Smartsheet, Procore, and Jonas, plus a live buyer question about whether Procore's own procurement extensions could substitute for a dedicated S2P platform.

competitors.name: Procore
competitors.quote: I am curious on because Procore has so many extensions and partnerships with companies. I'm wondering if they'd be able to do, like, almost like a plug in.
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as complementary depth on the procurement side rather than a competitor to Procore's project-management/accounting scope; Zycus itself acknowledged Procore's open API and offered to integrate rather than replace.

competitors.name: Status quo (Smartsheet + Jonas + point tools)
competitors.quote: Right now, we're using a system that's been designed... that's not actually designed for procurement. We just make it work.
competitors.how_we_win: Keep reinforcing the consolidation and compliance-automation value already articulated by the buyer himself; the risk is not a rival vendor but continued tolerance of the manual status quo given the 77-day silence.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Early-stage, single-threaded Qualified deal with one strong discovery call and real, buyer-voiced pain, but 105 days without stage movement, no economic buyer contact, and a cancelled follow-up that has not been rebooked.

deal_risk.text: 77 days of silence following a cancelled follow-up, against a 23 Sep 2026 close date, puts real pressure on whether this closes on the recorded timeline without immediate re-engagement.

No economic buyer, no second stakeholder, and no formal decision criteria or process on record: a $120k five-module deal at this stage needs more than one procurement manager's interest to survive a change in priorities or personnel.

win_position.text: Buyer pain is specific and buyer-voiced: Kyle Campbell described a fragmented, largely manual procurement stack (Smartsheet, 8 tools including Jonas) and asked detailed, engaged questions about AI-driven sourcing and union-vendor compliance filtering (call, 26 Mar 2026).

Zycus already delivered on both buyer asks (demo videos and ballpark pricing) the same day they were requested (email, 26 Mar 2026), so the ball is in the buyer's court with no outstanding Zycus deliverable.

No economic buyer or budget approver has had any contact, direct or indirect; Kyle Campbell said he would need to present internally to 'ownership' before this can move, and that step is unconfirmed 105 days later.

The only follow-up meeting ever scheduled (planned for 23 Apr 2026, pushed to 7 May 2026 at the buyer's request) was cancelled and never rebooked; the deal has had zero verified buyer contact in 77 days.
Single-threaded through one procurement manager with no contact role logged on the opportunity and no second stakeholder identified.

deal_momentum.text: Zero buyer engagement in the last 90-plus days; the last real touchpoint was the buyer's own request (23 Apr 2026 email) to push the meeting to 7 May 2026, and that meeting was then cancelled with nothing rescheduled since.
No future meeting, call, or dated milestone is currently on the calendar for this opportunity.
Next_Step__c has not been updated since it was written on 26 Mar 2026 ('follow-up meeting targeted in April'), a stale plan that predates two subsequent reschedules and a cancellation.

The one substantive call to date was high-depth (a full demo plus detailed Q&A on union compliance, Procore integration, and AI negotiation) and ended on a genuinely positive buyer note, so underlying interest has not visibly cooled, only gone quiet.

customer_commitment.text: Kyle Campbell asked to see the full workflow specifically so he could 'present it to ownership,' the clearest evidence of intent to advance the deal internally (call, 26 Mar 2026), but there is no confirmation this has happened.

The buyer proactively rescheduled rather than skipping the follow-up outright (23 Apr 2026 email), suggesting continued interest even amid a busy fiscal year-end, but the rescheduled 7 May 2026 session was still cancelled.

hard.ais_why:
• On the March 26 intro, Kyle Campbell engaged with the AI-driven RFI engine and autonomous negotiation pitch and asked about Procore integration for project-level spend.
• Bronte is a year-old procurement function running on Smartsheet, so the appetite is real but the org is small (25-30 users) and just starting to consolidate eight tools.
• A follow-up was placeheld for late April but the 4/23 call wasn't recorded; momentum exists but not yet at Hungry depth.

value.name: Procore
value.quote: I am curious on because Procore has so many extensions and partnerships with companies. I'm wondering if they'd be able to do, like, almost like a plug in.
value.how_we_win: Position Zycus as complementary depth on the procurement side rather than a competitor to Procore's project-management/accounting scope; Zycus itself acknowledged Procore's open API and offered to integrate rather than replace.

packets.subject: Procore

value.name: Status quo (Smartsheet + Jonas + point tools)
value.quote: Right now, we're using a system that's been designed... that's not actually designed for procurement. We just make it work.
value.how_we_win: Keep reinforcing the consolidation and compliance-automation value already articulated by the buyer himself; the risk is not a rival vendor but continued tolerance of the manual status quo given the 77-day silence.

packets.subject: Status quo (Smartsheet + Jonas + point tools)
